hindiTranslation: वे कहा करते हैं कि संसार असत्य, अप्रतिष्ठित और बिना ईश्वरके अपनेआप केवल स्त्रीपुरुषके संयोगसे पैदा हुआ है ।  इसलिये काम ही इसका कारण है, और कोई कारण नहीं है ॥
englishTranslation: They say the world is unreal, without foundation, and without a ruling order, born only from the union of male and female. They say desire alone is its cause, and nothing else.
essence: Cynical explanations of life can become a permission slip for harmful action.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: Some people deny any higher order in the world. They reduce existence to chance, physical union, and desire alone.
simpleInsight: A shallow explanation can feel confident and still miss what is most real.
detailedMeaning: On the battlefield, Krishna is exposing the mindset behind destructive action. After naming the traits of the asuric person, he shows how such people explain the world itself: no higher order, no moral foundation, only desire and accident. This prepares the warning in the next verse.
modernRelevance: A friend shrugs at betrayal and says, “People are just wired this way.” That sentence can sound clever, but it can also erase responsibility and flatten the whole moral world.
translationLiteral: असत्यम् (untrue) / अप्रतिष्ठम् (without foundation) / ते (they) / जगत् (world) / आहुः (say) / अनीश्वरम् (without a ruling lord) / अपरस्परसम्भूतम् (born without mutual cause) / किम् अन्यत् (what else) / कामहैतुकम् (caused by desire) ॥
